title: Cohort Definitions
description: Cohort Definitions allow researchers to specify inclusion and exclusion criteria using concept sets and logical rules to identify patient populations of interest. Cohorts form the foundational building blocks for analyses, patient profiles, and quality measures across the Parthenon platform.
tips: Use the Concept Set editor alongside your cohort definition to ensure all relevant clinical codes are captured across vocabularies.
Apply temporal logic (e.g., index event windows, inclusion criteria lookback periods) carefully to avoid inadvertent bias in cohort selection.
Validate cohort counts against expected prevalence estimates before using the cohort in downstream analyses.
Version your cohort definitions and document changes to maintain reproducibility across study iterations.
Reuse published cohort definitions from the Phenotype Library where available to accelerate study design.
